## Anti-pattern checklist

<!-- make sure your contribution meets the bar -->

- [ ] The code compiles (`ninja -C build` passes)
- [ ] The file opens with the four-field header block (BAD IDEA / WHY SOMEONE DOES IT / WHY IT'S TERRIBLE / HOW TO FIX IT)
- [ ] The bad code is recognizably bad, not randomly broken
- [ ] The bad code looks like something a real developer actually wrote
- [ ] `meson.build` updated to include the new file

## Commit message check

- [ ] My commit message would not pass a conventional-commits linter
- [ ] My commit message does not accurately describe what changed
- [ ] I have not referenced any trackable artifact (ticket, issue, PR)

<!-- anything else reviewers should know. optional. vague is fine. -->
